intro: Festuca irritans, also known as tall fescue, is a perennial grass species belonging to the Poaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Festuca irritans include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Festuca irritans is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to possess immunomodulatory and adaptogenic effects, promoting overall well-being and resilience.
